body: Football is a simple game. Twenty-two men chase a ball for four quarters of roughly twenty two and a half minutes in between pointless hydration breaks, and at the end, the Germans always win.

Ivory Coast conceded zero goals in the Afcon qualifiers and they won their last 4 games against France, Ecuador, Scotland and South Korea, conceding only 1 goal.

And yet we still made a comeback beating them. Great team, but we were better. We look good so far, WC is off to a great start but Nagelsmann has got to start Undav eventually.
Germany needs to improve their defence though, it was a mess this game... we were lucky that ivory coast couldnt finish. Felix Nmecha though, what a player
This wasn’t a great performance from Germany. But I do remember not that long ago, a time when we would have lost a game just like this one. So I’ll take it.
all our German things aside, Ivory coast is an amazing team.

I feel like im going crazy reading the amount of comments acting like Ivory coast controlled this game and got unlucky... 

They scored their one and only chance of the first half and luckily were able to hold onto the lead despite Germany carrying the play the majority of the game... IVC legitimately had two, mayyybe three chances in the whole game yet people are acting like the German defense was scrambling to contain them?

Sometimes I seriously question what match the people here actaully watched...

Great game from both teams. The intensity, the duels, the counters, the nerves... it really felt like a knockout game. 

Congratulations to Germany for their victory. Ivory Coast had countless opportunities to score a 2nd goal but unfortunately for them (fortunately for the Germans) they didn't. Adingra's miss was diabolical. 

When I saw Undav starting to enter the pitch, I knew he would be the X Factor and the turning point of that game. And he delivered ! What a game from him. 

Finally, it would be such a great moment to have Ivory Coast finally go through the GS. Even though their last game on paper against Curaçao should be a formality, we never know what could happen. Also I hope Singo's injury isn't that severe because that might be a huge blow for them. 

All in all, Ivory Coast really showed that they might be a tough team to beat and maybe be a last minute "dark horse" for this WC. They won against us (France) convincingly weeks ago in a friendly. They came back from behind and absolutely walked over us in the 2nd half (although we made several changes in that half). So I knew Ivory Coast would be a team to look for in this WC.

Was at the game, incredibly fun atmosphere 

Thought Germany was way too narrow at times and really improved once Sane came off. 

Ivory Coast played really well and it was far closer than I thought it would be

Also, the referee was awful

Ivory Coast very likely will be haunted by the chances they could not convert in front of the literal goal. They would not lose to Curacao and I doubt Germany would lose to Ecuador so they are very likely the second of the group. They will play runner up of France-Senegal-Norway group while Germany will most likely play a way weaker team. Thats veery unlucky seeding for IC, probably the worst group to pick your opponent from.

As a German, I have to give props to CIV, they looked solid af. Organized and structured on defense, fast and physical on offense. At times it seemed they have 1 man more, always a sign of good coaching & execution. I don’t think Germany played poorly at all and yet a 1-1 would’ve been fair. If they continue like that, I think they can beat Norway/Senegal and go the RO16.

No one talks about the real reason Ivory Coast were so good:
  
They have a trainer who understands the German game and countered it perfectly. 
IvC played a strong defense against Germanys strong offense and combined that with strong pressing and fast counters. They didn’t need many people to get chances because Germany placed most of the players high up. And Germany needed many people high up to break through IvC defense. And they stopped the Germans by marking man to man, it shut down a lot of germanies playstile. 

Both teams answered the other tactically and played to win. 

Curasao otoh also played an offensive game but got outclassed by Germany. They could have played defensively and would have condensed a lot less goals but without a chance. Like cap verde, park the bus.
It's a saying.

Being a German fan, I feel like IVC actually had a few more chances even later they COULD have but did not capitalize on, we did have some luck there. Our defence is still a bit leaky at times. I was also perplexed at the starting 11 choice, when you look at what a physical squad IVC is and happy when the subs finally came in. Much more physical players that the game demanded and we were finally rewarded for it. Nailbiter for sure.
Football is a cruel sport at times.

There was a time when I wouldn't have been able to enjoy a win like this, because it would've felt "too undeserved" or whatever. But this game wasn't like that, it was like an open brawl, and I'm really happy that Germany really got in the groove, accepted the physical intensity of the Ivorians and went with it, and got a scrappy win as a reward. Not a technical marvel of a game, but a hard fought win that required resilience and belief.

They also defended differently compared to their Ecuador game which fucked with Germanys game plan. I would also say that Musiala was the wrong player for this kind of opponent today.

IVC's best sustained stretch was from the opening 5 minutes of the 2nd half. Outside of that, they had a few random counters in the game that were dangerous but were largely on the defensive for the majority of the game.. The most notable run resulting from a terrible missed challenge by Tah that left 70% of the field open.

Clearly not Germany's best effort but the amount of time IVC were allowed to run through German players backs on the defensive side was a bit wild. The fact that Germany was able to pull this out with so much going against them really is a testament to the teams depth.
Having the tied-most World Cups in your continent. And second-most ever.. You can make a rough parallel, LOL.

None of the disallowed goals were a stroke of luck.  
Those were goals as a result of blatant fouls. 

Stroke of luck would be if goal gets disallowed by millimeter offsides or some soft inconsequential foul.
Of course, more like the 2014 Algeria, not like the 2026 Algeria.

Germany will not necessarily face the 3rd place team from that particular group. I guess they can’t be that definitive because they don’t know in advance which 8 of the 12 third place teams will actually advance.

I don’t know how they are even saying some of the second place matchups are definite, because some second place teams will face other second place teams, but 4 of them should be matched up with group winners.

Either way, though, Ivory Coast’s path definitely got immensely harder with the loss. That’s a shame, because they are a fun watch.
